name: Imhotep
shortDescription: Imhotep was an Egyptian polymath, physician, and high priest, later deified as a god of medicine and healing. He is considered one of the earliest known physicians and architects in history.
description: Imhotep was a prominent figure in ancient Egyptian history, revered as a polymath, physician, architect, and high priest of Ra at Heliopolis. Living during the Third Dynasty (c. 2667–2600 BCE), specifically under the reign of Pharaoh Djoser, Imhotep is credited with designing the Step Pyramid of Djoser at Saqqara, one of the earliest monumental stone structures in Egypt. His skills in medicine were legendary, and he is often considered the founder of Egyptian medicine, with some scholars suggesting he authored the Edwin Smith Papyrus, a surgical textbook containing detailed anatomical observations and treatments. Unlike many other deities, Imhotep was a historical figure who was later deified. His rise to divine status began centuries after his death, during the New Kingdom period (c. 1550–1070 BCE), and he eventually became associated with healing and medicine. Temples were built in his honor, and he was often invoked by those seeking cures. Imhotep's influence extended beyond Egypt; he was sometimes equated with the Greek god Asclepius. As a deity, he was seen as a benevolent protector and healer, embodying wisdom, knowledge, and the power of medicine. His legacy endures as a testament to the remarkable achievements of ancient Egyptian civilization.

sources.title: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.author: Wilkinson, Richard H.
